headline: PepsiCo (PEP) Valuation Check After Earnings Beat Dividend Hike And Health Focused Product Push
summary: PepsiCo (PEP) has drawn fresh attention after beating recent quarterly expectations, announcing a 4% dividend increase, and advancing its health focused snack lineup with the new PopCorners Protein range. See our latest analysis for PepsiCo. Despite the upbeat earnings surprise and dividend increase, PepsiCo’s recent share price momentum has been weak, with the stock down 10.1% on a 30 day share price return and 14.2% over 90 days, even as the 1 year total shareholder return sits at 12.4%...

headline: PepsiCo vs. Celsius: Which Consumer Goods Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
summary: One company boasts global stability and strong cash flow, while the other posts rapid growth but faces higher risk. Their latest numbers reveal distinct paths.

headline: How PepsiCo is Achieving Affordability
summary: Speaking at the Wall Street Journal Global Food Forum in Chicago, PepsiCo’s Mike Del Pozzo shares the strategies his company is using to keep their products affordable without sacrificing innovation.

headline: Will Pepsi Stock Hit a New All Time High in 2026?
summary: The question of whether PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) can punch through to a new all-time high in 2026 is closer to a coin flip than the chart suggests. Shares trade at $144.19, sitting roughly 1% below the 52-week high of $169.96, but the stock has lost 7.15% over the last month. Our proprietary model frames that pullback ... Will Pepsi Stock Hit a New All Time High in 2026?

headline: Plot Raises $10M to Scale Social Video Intelligence for Marketing Teams at Fortune 500 Brands
summary: Plot, the social video intelligence platform trusted by Visa, Mastercard, PepsiCo, Fenty Beauty, Benefit Cosmetics, and CAVA, today announced $10M in new funding to meet enterprise demand for its expanding platform. The seed round brings Plot's total raised to $14M and was co-led by XYZ Venture Capital and Mischief Ventures, with participation from Seven Seven Six and Acme Capital.

headline: Should Celsius’ PepsiCo Expansion and Alani Nu Deal Require Action From Celsius Holdings (CELH) Investors?
summary: In recent months, Celsius Holdings outlined at the CAGNY conference how its multi-brand strategy, US$1.80 billion Alani Nu acquisition, and expanded PepsiCo distribution partnership are intended to accelerate its footprint in the fast-growing functional beverage space. The company framed these moves as a way to align closely with a structural consumer shift toward zero-sugar, functional energy drinks, reinforcing its role in reshaping the energy beverage category. We will now examine how...
